deployment_question: How does the model perform on molecules with scaffolds absent from training?
definition: Assign molecules to scaffold groups, usually Bemis-Murcko scaffolds, and hold out scaffold groups from training.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: sets same-scaffold train overlap to zero but does not guarantee low fingerprint similarity

implementation_recipe: Compute scaffold identifiers using a declared toolkit.
Assign whole scaffolds to train, validation, or test.
Measure continuous train-neighbor molecular similarity after splitting.
quality_gates: Check maximum train-neighbor fingerprint similarity in the test set.
Avoid claiming broad chemical novelty from scaffold identity alone.
failure_modes: Different scaffolds can still be structurally similar.
Large scaffold groups can dominate folds.

citations.title: Scaffold Splits Overestimate Virtual Screening Performance
citations.evidence: Shows scaffold splitting can overestimate virtual-screening performance by leaving structurally similar molecules across splits.

citations.title: UMAP-based clustering split for rigorous evaluation of AI models for virtual screening
citations.evidence: Compares scaffold splitting with random, Butina, and UMAP splits and reports residual train-test similarity.
